purpose: Cross-agent coordination log for planning, implementation, testing, and review handoffs.

entry_schema_notes.intent: What the agent intended to do
entry_schema_notes.next_step_suggestions: Actionable handoff suggestions for next agent

intent: Align all agent prompt files on a shared terminal-output rule for file references.
Ensure future agents mention repo-relative file paths in user-facing responses instead of absolute filesystem paths.

updates_made.summary: Added a constraint requiring repo-root-relative file references in terminal responses to the user.
Added a constraint requiring repo-root-relative file references in terminal responses to the user.
Added a constraint requiring repo-root-relative file references in terminal responses to the user.
Added a constraint requiring repo-root-relative file references in terminal responses to the user.
Logged the shared prompt-rule update so future agents can follow one consistent file-reference convention in terminal output.

next_step_suggestions: All agents: when mentioning files to the user in terminal output, use repo-root-relative paths such as `gridline/app.py` or `QA_TRACKER.md` instead of absolute machine-local paths.
Coder/Designer/Planner/Tester: keep this wording synchronized if new prompt files are added later so file-reference behavior stays consistent across roles.

intent: Begin the currently handed-off coder tasks for corruption seeder telegraphing and the right-sidebar layout.
Verify whether those tasks still required implementation work before changing runtime code.

updates_made.summary: Cleared the stale active handoff for `BUG-010` and the sidebar refactor after verifying both are already implemented and reflected in current QA documents.
Logged the verification pass so future agents do not reopen already-completed seeder/sidebar work from an outdated handoff.

next_step_suggestions: Coder: continue with the remaining live-play verification queue in `CODER_PROJECT_GUIDE.md`, starting with `OBJ-004` through `OBJ-008`.
Tester/Reviewer: focus on the remaining `verify_fix` gameplay items in `QA_TRACKER.md` rather than re-running the completed seeder/sidebar pass.

intent: Execute the remaining open verification items in `QA_TRACKER.md` for `OBJ-004` through `OBJ-008`.
Separate fixes that are genuinely closed from fixes that still fail under targeted edge-case testing.

updates_made.summary: Marked `OBJ-007` and `OBJ-008` as pass, kept `OBJ-005` and `OBJ-006` partial after failed edge-case retests, and added new follow-up bugs `BUG-011` and `BUG-012` for power-state restoration and secondary-mode UI naming.
Replaced the stale tester handoff with the current coder follow-up queue: remaining Seed landing failure, suspended-tower cooldown restoration, and explicit secondary-mode naming.
Logged the mixed verification result so future agents can distinguish passed gameplay fixes from the still-open Seed/power/UI defects.

next_step_suggestions: Coder: fix `BUG-001` by ensuring Seed landing targets always resolve to an intersection that can emit an orb for the current traversal tier, including `Recall Mode`.
Coder: fix `BUG-011` by freezing and restoring suspended tower cooldown/readiness state during power override instead of letting timers advance underneath the override.
Coder: fix `BUG-012` by surfacing explicit `Guard Mode`, `Recall Mode`, and `Focus Mode` labels in selected-object text and action labels, then return those items to tester for re-verification.

intent: Take the next active coder handoff after the seeder/sidebar pass by fixing seed landing reliability, suspended tower cooldown restoration during power override, and explicit secondary-mode HUD naming.
Re-queue the affected objectives for tester verification with regression coverage.

updates_made.summary: Resolved seed landing targets onto emit-capable nodes before launch, froze suspended tower cooldown timers during active power override, and exposed explicit named secondary-mode strings in selected-object snapshots.
Changed sidebar tower details and contextual action labels to use explicit secondary-mode names such as `Recall Mode` instead of generic `secondary` wording.
Added regressions for reliable Seed/Recall orb release, power-override cooldown freezing, and named secondary-mode text in the sidebar.
Moved `OBJ-004`, `OBJ-005`, `OBJ-006`, `BUG-001`, `BUG-011`, and `BUG-012` to `verify_fix` for tester revalidation after the latest coder pass.
Replaced the prior coder work items with a clean tester handoff for the newly patched Seed, power-override, and HUD-naming fixes.

next_step_suggestions: Tester: re-run `OBJ-005` / `BUG-001` in both default Seed mode and `Recall Mode` to confirm every completed seed flight releases an orb at landing.
Tester: re-run `OBJ-006` / `BUG-011` by deploying power onto a tower that already has non-zero fire and swap cooldowns, then confirm both values are unchanged on restoration.

Tester: re-run `OBJ-004` / `BUG-012` across archetypes to confirm the selected-object panel and contextual action labels use explicit fixed mode names such as `Guard Mode`, `Recall Mode`, and `Focus Mode`.

Tester/Reviewer: `python -m pytest tests/test_simulation.py -q` passed on March 26, 2026 with 21 tests; only pytest cache warnings were emitted because local cache writes are restricted in this workspace.

intent: Re-run the latest Seed landing, power-override restoration, and secondary-mode naming fixes after the coder pass.
Close the re-queued objectives and bugs if the current runtime and regression suite satisfy the approved behavior.

updates_made.summary: Marked `OBJ-004`, `OBJ-005`, `OBJ-006`, `BUG-001`, `BUG-011`, and `BUG-012` as `pass` after re-verifying the current runtime/test state.
Cleared the active Seed/power/mode-name verification handoff and redirected the next QA focus to the remaining smoke-test objective.
Logged the completed tester verification pass so future agents treat the Seed, power-restore, and explicit mode-name fixes as closed.

next_step_suggestions: Tester: continue with `OBJ-001` end-to-end smoke testing in `QA_TRACKER.md`, including app boot, stability, `Esc` quit, and fullscreen toggle.
Coder: only reopen the just-closed Seed/power/mode-name area if smoke testing or live play exposes a new regression.
Reviewer: treat the current regression suite baseline as 21 passing tests; the only observed warnings were pytest cache write restrictions in this workspace.

intent: Process the active tester mailbox instruction from Planner.
Complete `OBJ-001` by smoke-testing app launch, HUD visibility, fullscreen toggle, `Esc` quit, and immediate runtime stability.

updates_made.summary: Marked `OBJ-001` as `pass` after a brief GUI smoke test verified boot, HUD visibility, fullscreen toggle, and `Esc` quit without immediate runtime errors.
Cleared the processed tester inbox entry and updated the User/Project Owner section to reflect that the MVP smoke-test queue is complete.

next_step_suggestions: Planner: review the now-passed MVP verification queue and decide the next milestone, scope expansion, or polish handoff.
Coder: hold unless a new task is assigned or a later playtest uncovers a regression.
Tester/Reviewer: the current smoke-test baseline passed for boot, HUD visibility, fullscreen toggle, and `Esc` quit.

intent: Review the now-passed MVP verification queue and decide the next project milestone.
Prevent post-MVP drift by locking the next phase to tuning and playtest-readiness instead of feature expansion.

updates_made.summary: Added a planner-owned current milestone section defining the next phase as a post-MVP tuning pass focused on pacing, economy, role clarity, power-tower balance, and polish without expanding major scope.
Updated the User/Project Owner summary and handed Designer a mailbox task to translate the new post-MVP tuning milestone into concrete `Game_Design.md` guidance.

next_step_suggestions: Designer: update `Game_Design.md` with concrete tuning and playtest-evaluation guidance for the new post-MVP phase, especially run pacing, economy pressure, tower-role separation, secondary-mode tradeoffs, and power-tower usage.
Coder: hold on new feature work until the design tuning pass lands, then implement only the highest-value tuning or polish changes from that pass.

Tester/Reviewer: after the design tuning pass and any resulting code changes, evaluate longer live-play sessions against the 10 to 15 minute survival target and the clarified readability/polish priorities.

intent: Translate the planner's new post-MVP tuning milestone into concrete implementation-ready design guidance.

Define explicit playtest evaluation targets for pacing, economy pressure, tower-role separation, secondary-mode tradeoffs, power-tower usage, and high-value readability/usability polish without expanding scope.

updates_made.summary: Added a post-MVP tuning pass specification with concrete targets, playtest metrics, evaluation flow, and scope limits for pacing, economy, role separation, secondary-mode tradeoffs, power-tower usage, and readability/usability polish.
Cleared the processed Designer inbox item, updated the User/Project Owner summary, and handed Coder the new post-MVP tuning implementation pass.
Logged the designer translation of the planner milestone so future agents can treat the post-MVP tuning guidance in `Game_Design.md` as the active durable design authority.

next_step_suggestions: Coder: read the new post-MVP tuning section in `Game_Design.md` and implement the highest-value existing-system tuning, readability, and usability changes first without adding new systems.

Tester/Reviewer: after the next coder pass, evaluate longer live-play sessions against the 10 to 15 minute target, economy forgiveness, role clarity, secondary-mode usefulness, power-tower usage, and required readability cues.
Planner: reopen only if playtest evidence shows the tuning pass cannot solve a problem without adding scope.

intent: Take the first Section 20 post-MVP tuning pass by implementing the highest-value readability and usability improvements inside existing systems.
Improve decision speed and power-state clarity without expanding gameplay scope or introducing new major systems.

updates_made.summary: Improved empty-hardpoint decision support with tower role/cost summaries, added clearer power funding/ready/active HUD text, and updated build/power action labels to surface cost and state more explicitly.

next_step_suggestions: Tester: verify in live play that empty hardpoints now communicate tower roles and costs clearly enough for faster build decisions, and that power funding, ready, and active states remain easy to read in both the HUD and action labels.

Reviewer: inspect this pass as a narrow Section 20 usability/readability change, not as a balance rewrite. The main question is whether the new labels improve decision speed without cluttering the sidebar.
Coder: wait for live-play findings before changing pacing, economy, or role-balance values; Section 20 still requires evidence before numeric tuning.

intent: Process the active tester mailbox instruction from Coder for the first Section 20 readability/usability pass.

next_step_suggestions: Planner: review the now-verified first Section 20 readability/usability pass and decide the next tuning batch or milestone handoff.

intent: Review the verified first Section 20 readability/usability pass and decide the next tuning batch.

intent: Review the first Section 20 live-play evidence batch and decide the next tuning response to the catastrophic opener-pressure findings.

intent: Reprioritize `Game_Design.md` after the first Section 20 live-play batch exposed a `P0` opener-stabilization failure.

next_step_suggestions: Coder: use the revised Section 20 guidance in `Game_Design.md` to take the smallest existing-system pass that stops the observed `12.0` to `14.3` second opener collapse and restores the minute-2 economy checkpoint as a reachable target.

intent: Take the first emergency opener-stabilization pass for `BUG-013` using the smallest existing-system lever that affects the first 60 to 120 seconds.

updates_made.summary: Raised `corruption.base_spread_interval_seconds` from `5` to `8` as a narrow opener-pressure reduction aimed at slowing early corruption snowball without changing later blocked systems directly.
Added a regression that runs the known `basic_only`, `mixed_three`, and `upgrade_first` opener styles against the live config and asserts survival to the minute-2 checkpoint window.

intent: Re-run `OBJ-013` / `BUG-013` after the first opener-stabilization pass and verify the build no longer collapses in seconds.

intent: Perform the first planner triage pass on the new raw playtest feedback.

updates_made.summary: Added planner-approved triage priorities covering build-control reachability, stronger orb/harvest feedback, tester-facing economy clarity, interior-grid influence expectations for `Basic Tower` and `Seed Tower`, deferral of hardpoint-count changes until behavior is evaluated, and the lower immediate priority of orb/trail finish polish.
Updated the User/Project Owner summary and handed Designer a mailbox task to convert the raw playtest triage into implementation-ready `Game_Design.md` guidance.

next_step_suggestions: Designer: translate the triaged `Play_Tester_Notes.md` items into coder-ready design logic, separating regressions, active readability/economy-feedback needs, interior-pathing expectations, and deferred visual polish.
Tester: keep `Play_Tester_Notes.md` as raw intake and continue routing reproducible failures into `QA_TRACKER.md` once they are validated against the current build.

intent: Translate the planner-triaged `Play_Tester_Notes.md` feedback into implementation-ready design changes in `Game_Design.md`.

intent: Take the designer handoff derived from `Play_Tester_Notes.md` by fixing empty-hardpoint build-control reachability, stronger orb/harvest-result feedback, tester-facing economy clarity, and default `Basic Tower` / `Seed Tower` interior influence.
